get.description: Size of the Argus data directory in bytes.

Discovery manifest so clients can adapt instead of hardcoding assumptions:
which detection types are usable right now, supported formats, pagination limits,
and which integration features this build exposes.

post.description:
Batch detect across multiple images.

Multipart: one or more ``file`` fields, optional ``type`` field, optional
``external_refs`` field (JSON array of strings, parallel to files by index).

JSON (preferred): ``{"images": [{"url": "...", "external_ref": "...?"}, ...],
"type": "faces|objects|all"}``

JSON (legacy): ``{"image_urls": [...], "type": "faces|objects|all"}``
(external_ref will be null for every item)

1:1 face verification — are the two supplied images the same person?

Two images, each as exactly one of file{n}/image{n}_url/image{n}_base64.
Stores nothing. Uses the highest-confidence face in each image.

1:N identification — who is each face, among this user's enrolled people?

One image (file/image_url/image_base64). Read-only: stores nothing. Returns the
best match per face (null below threshold, with the best-guess similarity) plus a
ranked suggestion list.

Stateless detection — is there a person / object in this image?

One image (file/image_url/image_base64). Pure detection: runs the face and
object engines and returns bboxes + counts. Stores nothing, enrolls nothing,
matches nothing. ``?type=faces|objects|all`` (default all) selects which
engines run; an engine that isn't loaded is simply skipped (its list is
empty and ``available`` is false), so the call never 503s on a missing model.

Stateless batch detection — multiple images in one call, stores nothing.

multipart/form-data: repeat `file` for each image (plus optional `type`).
application/json: ``{"type": "...", "image_urls": [...], "image_base64": [...]}``.
Per-image results — one bad image never fails the rest. Mirrors /api/detect/bulk
but read-only (no source images, detections, crops, or matching).

Compare faces in a reference image against one or more target images.

Detects faces in all images and finds matches using cosine similarity.
Read-only — stores nothing.

Multipart: reference_file (one) + target_file (one or more).
Also accepts reference_url/reference_base64 and target_urls/target_base64 (JSON arrays).
JSON body: reference_url or reference_base64, plus target_urls or target_base64 arrays.

Match all unidentified face detections against enrolled identities.
Returns counts of detections scanned and newly matched.

get.description: Aggregate counts for the dashboard — single round-trip.
Identities with counts + thumbnail in one query — for paginated dashboard grid.

post.description:
Merge identity {identity_id} into {body.into}: all detections and embeddings are
reassigned to the target, then the source identity is deleted.

Batch read: fetch current state of many detections in one call. For clients
reconciling stored records against Argus without N round-trips. Unknown/foreign
ids are simply absent from the result.
Promote a confirmed detection's embedding to the identity's reference set.

delete.description: Remove this detection's crop from the identity's reference set (inverse of enroll).

get.description: List reference embeddings for an identity (without the raw vectors).

post.description: Create a new face identity and store its first embedding in one call.
Add a reference embedding to an existing face identity.

put.description: Hot-swap the active engine. Synchronous — loads from disk if not cached.

get.description: All settings grouped by category. Values are type-coerced.

face.description: Minimum cosine similarity to accept a face match

example.description: Minimum cosine similarity to accept a face match
Minimum cosine similarity to accept a face match

post.description: Reset one key (?key=…) or an entire category (?category=…) to defaults.

example.description: Minimum cosine similarity to accept a face match

get.description: Return the buffered log lines (oldest first). Optional ?level= and ?limit=.
List available on-disk log dates, newest first.
Return a paginated slice of the app log for the given UTC date.
Return a paginated slice of the activity log for the given UTC date.

delete.description: Delete both log files (app + activity) for the given UTC date.

get.description:
Confirmed face detections whose embedding scores poorly against their
identity's representative — possible mislabels, sorted worst-first. See Mismatches tab.

post.description:
Mark a Mismatches-tab detection as reviewed-and-correct. Suppresses it from
future mismatch scans without affecting its gallery presence or label.

Batch confirm-as-correct for the Mismatches tab. Sets mismatch_reviewed on each
detection, suppressing them from future scans without affecting gallery or labels.

Clear identity and return to unidentified queue (re-label path).
Distinct from reject: reject marks the match wrong but keeps the identity link.

200.description: JPEG crop image for this detection

post.description:
Relabel many detections in one call. Per-item results — one bad item never
fails the others. Same contract as the single PUT label endpoint.

Hide detections from Suggested people without deleting them (sets an ignored flag).
The rows remain visible on the tag page and in the image's data.
Permanently delete detections and their crop files. For junk crops you never want.

get.description:
Paginated list of processed source images.
Optional filters: identity_id (repeatable, AND semantics), type (face/object),
since, until, external_ref, no_detections, no_tagged_faces, has_manual_detections.
sort: newest (default), oldest, most_detections, fewest_detections, last_modified.

schema.description: Filter by detection type: face or object

schema.description: ISO timestamp — images uploaded at or after

schema.description: ISO timestamp — images uploaded at or before

get.description: Total count of source images matching the given filters.
All source image IDs matching the given filters (no pagination, for select-all).

delete.description:
Delete a source image and cascade-delete all its detections (faces + objects).

Use this before re-detecting a photo to avoid duplicate detections. References
enrolled from the removed crops are dropped too, so no orphaned references remain.

post.description:
Re-run detection on an already-stored source image using the currently active models.

Query params:
  type=faces|objects|all  (default: all)
  replace=true            (clear existing detections of that type first; default: false)
  async=true              (return a job_id and process in background; default: false)

Save a manually-drawn bounding box as a face detection.

Attempts InsightFace recognition on the crop; if no face is found, the
detection is saved without an embedding (label only). Requires exactly one
of ``label`` or ``identity_id``.

get.description:
Group unlabeled face detections into suggested people. Read-only: computes
clusters on demand, stores nothing. Name a cluster by POSTing its detection_ids
to /api/detections/label with the same label.

Number of suggested-people groups, for the nav notification dot. Uses the
default grouping threshold.

200.description: Zip archive of exported identity data
